page: Amazon Web Services x8i.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64 and includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multi-core and memory-intensive workloads. The instance achieves top 10% placement in multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU score, and large-block memory bandwidth read/write tests. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in database operations, processing 274.54 million Redis SET operations per second, and shows strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. However, single-core CPU performance and Passmark memory score fall into the average tier.

The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, meaning all storage must be network-attached, and GPU-accelerated workloads are not supported. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of DDR5 memory and dedicated compute resources positions this instance in a premium hardware class, optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy enterprise workloads, large-scale software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor offering 192 vCPUs (96 physical cores) at 3.9 GHz, alongside 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. The architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX). Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, large-block memory bandwidth, and database operations, including Redis SET performance. However, it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. This high-density memory configuration is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 192 vCPUs, 3 TB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 3072 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 3 TB of DDR5 RAM for demanding in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors.
3072.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (16 GB per core).
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks.
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs.
Suited for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
